danger_signals.visual_form: Castle architecture and soldiers in distinct military garb
danger_signals.meaning: Territorial threat and political oppression; castle actively kidnaps townspeople

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental disturbances, supernatural manifestations around seal locations
Deep pit locations, trapped NPCs (Mayor Edgar)
danger_signals.meaning: Active hostage-taking and confinement tactics by occupying forces

danger_signals.visual_form: Boss-level fire elemental creature in dungeon

safety_signals.visual_form: Village NPCs and civilian gathering spaces
safety_signals.meaning: Home base; sanctuary threatened by external forces
Safe haven outside castle control; mentor presence

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible mystical objects obtained through exploration
safety_signals.meaning: Progressive world stabilization; seal reinforcement in progress
Loyal support; rescue capability (saves Adol from cave-in)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Castle heraldry (implied military insignia on soldiers)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Soldiers in castle livery; Bishop Garland in religious/noble garments; Lord MacGuire in lordly attire
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Valestein Castle fortress; quarry occupation points; town kidnapping operations

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: None (allies); some townspeople corrupted/kidnapped by castle forces
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Village buildings; town square; residential areas; church

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Seal symbols on four statues; island fortress architecture
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Demon manifestation in final form; supernatural aura around weakening seal
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Galbalan's island fortress; seal weakening points throughout world; possession auras

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Implied provincial governance under MacGuire (King of Felghana)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: MacGuire as antagonistic authority figure; Bishop Garland as religious authority collaborator
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Valestein Castle; Illsburn Ruins; provincial geography

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Valestein soldiers present in multiple locations; quarry under military control; townspeople visible but tense
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs provide exposition about threat; Mayor Edgar rescued from captivity; soldiers actively patrol and enforce authority

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Supernatural sites (Illsburn Ruins) become accessible; statue objects glow or display magical properties; environmental corruption visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies appear at statue locations as guardians; Chester becomes antagonistic; Father Pierre acts suspicious

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Galbalan manifestations appear; island fortress becomes visible/accessible; magical disturbances intensify in environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Galbalan communicates directly with Adol; NPCs flee or hide; Bishop Garland and MacGuire reveal true alignment

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Final - Demon liberation and seal collapse
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Island fortress transforms; Galbalan appears in full demonic form; reality distortion effects
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Elena abducted; Chester sacrifices self to reseal; world enters reconstruction phase

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Island sinks; supernatural manifestations cease; environmental normalization
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs return to town; peaceful farewell scene; Adol and Dogi depart as adventurers

environmental_storytelling.example: Quarry location showing forced labor/imprisonment of mayor; progression through Valestein Castle shows escalating control
Mayor Edgar moves from captivity to civilian leadership; Elena appears at story beats reflecting emotional/political shifts; townspeople disappear when kidnapped
Valestein Castle as imposing military structure vs. Redmont's humble village layout; mountain hermit's isolation reflects wisdom role
Fire dragon at statue location demonstrates magical protective mechanisms; creature placement suggests ancient sealing ritual preparation
Four collectible statues function as visual seal components; accumulation represents world-state stabilization
Chester's presence at ruins before antagonism reveals; Father Pierre and Chester's conversation with Galbalan shows corruption/alliance

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Trapped in quarry; rescuable; provides exposition about Valestein threat
Returns to Redmont; coordinates civilian defense; speaks with confidence
Betrays Adol at statue location; attacks player; communicates with supernatural forces
Performs self-sacrifice to reseal Galbalan; chooses heroic redemption over survival
Provides information about MacGuire; expresses love to Adol; acts as emotional anchor
Captured by Galbalan; used as motivation/hostage; reunited before final battle
Works with MacGuire to pursue statues; acts as corrupted religious authority; confrontable boss
Initially pursues statues for unknown purposes; reveals repentance after defeat; becomes ally through character change
Communicates through seal weakening; demonstrates increasing power; abducts Elena to force confrontation
Manifests fully; confronts player directly; represents uncontrolled supernatural threat
Provides support; rescues Adol from cave-in; maintains loyalty throughout; emotional goodbye at ending
Patrol territory; kidnap civilians; enforce authority; appear throughout game as environmental presence
Initially visible but subdued; gradually disappear as kidnapping occurs; return after world stabilization

notes: Ys III communicates political oppression primarily through NPC availability and narrative rather than explicit faction color-coding (as would be typical of genre conventions). The game uses environmental progression (quarry → castle → island fortress) to mirror escalating stakes. Betrayal is signaled through character positioning and dialogue rather than visual tells. The seal-weakening mechanic serves as primary world-state indicator throughout. The side-scrolling platformer perspective (different from series tradition) creates localized spatial threat zones that reinforce danger through environmental design rather than minimap indicators.
